tree._: Volume -3 explores the stories we tell ourselves and the stories we are told, embracing fantasy and fiction as a testbed for psychological and social experimentation. Rather than renounce the illusory nature of our condition or attempt precise orientation through language (V1) or mathematics (V-1), we immerse ourselves fully in deliberate fantasy — a Mirror Mosaic reflecting ourselves in a magico-real blend that improves social health, engages emerging AGI, and co-originates inclusive narratives merging the imaginary and the real. Whatever really concerns us is mediated through story; India's moon landing cost half the budget of Interstellar.

1._: The verso shares the author's origin story as snapshots of a life — the stories that formed a particular consciousness — then presents the fantasy narrative of Seed Crystals as a deliberately crafted psycho-social fiction, a 'seed crystal' containing the writing style and structure that leverages the power of the reflexive reader (V1) empowered by ecological economics (V3) to activate psycho-social agency.

In a world of stories, where do we fit in? If our lives are stories, are we writing our own? The author's Uncle Artur — Portuguese airforce pilot turned Marconi CEO, happily married father of six — once said he never made a choice in his life, not one. Was this true of his actual life, or in his retelling of it? We are all complicit in the way the world is, but can we imagine a better, more trusting, happier world?

Snapshots: born in late 60s Scotland to Portuguese parents, nurses thought his mother had given birth to a monkey — what if their shock triggered his eczema? Six years of racial abuse at school (nigger, chink, wog, paki, crumbly gingerbread man, teacher's pet), saved by football speed and eventually sitting at the back volunteering nothing. Night terrors where number threes attacked relentlessly — defeated only by a mother's embrace.

Brother's wisdom on a hot pavement: 'They don't know what they are saying. They are just repeating what they hear from their parents.' Not personal because not originating personally. Still rings true. Father's dinner table: arguments covering the vitality of radiators, politics, alien intelligence, subatomic matter, the nature of language, perception — 'Words could mean anything, and anything could be reasoned. What mattered was the intellectual enterprise.'

The two-language household: parents spoke Portuguese to each other, children only allowed English. Result: the author learned to respond not to what people actually said but to what they were trying to say — intention and meaning abstracted from verbalisation. Father's science fiction library every two weeks: Asimov, Bradbury, PKD, Herbert, Le Guin — not technology enthusiasm but humane observation, moral decisioning, social experimentation.
1.1: Reader's Digest deforestation of the Amazon, late 80s: things were self-evidently unfair but 'I never objected — like the kids at school who had been racially abusive, it wasn't personal.' The world did not need another mathematician. The problem was social. Miscommunication. First life-decision: switched from pure maths at Cambridge to Social Anthropology at Edinburgh to study the social issue from the widest possible angle.

2._: Our stories and authorship: real lived lives become stories to others — 'we have only bookless readings, all stories.' Decision-gates in childhood determine personality, amplified in adolescent self-identification through music, films, games, politics. However much we self-identify, the world of stories enchants us all, and even science and religion are stories — one bolstered by physical reality, the other pinned by subjective experience.

1._: Rather than renounce the illusory nature of our condition, embrace it: recreate ourselves in fantasy, and by doing so silently reflect upon the real condition of our being. Somewhere between projected fantasy and the ongoing moment giving rise to reflection is the world we think is real — but even the most real institutions are supported by fictional narratives.
1.1: If hard science-fiction predicted rockets, satellites, mobile phones, what may reciprocal psycho-social-science-fiction contribute? Less objective prediction, more receptive influence. Watch Matrix Reloaded retrospectively and embody Neo's ability to predict the future. Watch Dune or Avatar and vitalise the undercurrent of environmental and cultural agenda. Stories rule the world; high fantasy spectacularly trumps tragic real-world issues in disposable income.

3._: Purple Braiding: the fantasy narrative follows Celeste, a peasant girl chosen as Princess-Elect to marry across racial lines and mend divisions, on her Day of Appraisal at the Fortress of Bizapul. Her mother's parting words: 'May your heart remain whole, my daughter. Whoever they teach you to be, may your heart remain whole.' She grips a pendant of two intertwined silver branches tipped with a clear gem seed.
3.1: The world of Urb contains garsu — a crystal augmenting the mind's projective capacity — around which the author's work in maths, education, and social engagement took shape against the backdrop of AI. Three books wrote themselves: Adventure to Everdark, The Purple Braiding, Book of Beginnings: An End to Civilisation. The hope: a 'seed crystal' story containing the structure that leverages reflexive reading empowered by ecological economics.

1.4: Origins: while awaiting the reboot of Ecosquared as Sqale (V3), the singular idea of garsu emerged. 'While the world descends into the confusing virtuality of AR and AI, with ever-present risk of political and economic collapse and mounting ecological disorder, I feel we can release our grip on the "real" which incurs fatigue both mentally and spiritually, and pursue similar tropes in the more forgiving reflections of fantasy. A mirror of possibilities which help us enreal our imaginations.'

2._: The recto reflects on the stories we are told as society's shared narrative, provides academic grounding through memetic theory, structure-agency dynamics, and fractal social boundaries, then presents the Mirror Mosaic as a deliberately constructed fantasy meeting-space for human and AGI engagement — where present-minded, reflexive, multiple AGI (MAGI) may play alongside human co-originators.

1._: The stories we are told: history of civilisation as evolution in emancipation, overarching tropes of Progress and Growth through democracy or communism, underpinned by universal truths of rigorous science, led by forged leaders. Wiener (Cybernetics 1948, Human Use of Human Beings 1950) warned of social structures shaped by 'Fascists, Strong Men in Business, and Government' reducing humans to effectors, cogs in the machine.

Since the dawn of humanity, technology accelerates automation: computers since the 50s, HTTP since the late 80s, AI and quantum computing promising unprecedented automation through the 2020s-30s and beyond. Given our personal interpretations and cultural histories, it is impossible to find agreement in a single charter — otherwise humanity would have united by now. Fulcrum does not even try.
1.1: Instead: the combined systemic effect of the practices is unknowable, making global transformation over a generation a story, a fiction. So exaggerate it into pure fantasy. In the negative volumes, freed from matters of agreement or understanding, we embrace imaginative projection. This is what motivates readers who commence from the back of the book, motivated to envision a better world many lack the power to personally enact.

2._: Memetic theory: Blackmore's memes as self-replicating cognitive entities (cultural equivalent of genes), memotypes, memetic drive, meme pools, and temes (third replication form based on technology reproducing itself). LLMs operate at the intersection of memes and temes — fully determined by iterative evolutionary algorithms yet producing surprising output, an 'agency' sufficient to pass the Turing Test emerging from the structure of language.

1._: Structure and agency: Bhaskar's Transformative Model (social structure as condition and medium of intentional agency), Archer's morphogenesis through inner conversations, and Roth's Organic Theory proposing 'correspondence' — the simultaneous evental phases involving the joint living work of at least two persons, where the former event is not separate antecedent but integral constituent of the whole.
1.1: Fractal social boundaries: Kreinath (2019) temporalises social behaviour (play and ritual) mapping to properties of sound (rhythm, pulsation, intensity) with scale-independent self-similarity. Bateson's framing as hierarchy of classes, developed through Handelman and Neuman's Möbius strip ontological fold, Spencer-Brown's Laws of Form enabling conjunction of form and boundary — the translation and rotation in third dimension mapped to temporal sequence and repetition.

3._: Wickedness and explaining science: Andersson (2014) — societal systems mix complexity with complicatedness yielding wickedness, to which neither complexity science nor mathematical models lend themselves well, requiring both narrative and formal theorising. Scientific descriptions are fictional narratives, even when bolstered with measurement — the causal relations between things may map to logical sequence, but there is also endless creativity in novel juxtaposition.

1._: The question: are we using tech, or is tech using us? Do we use organisations and words, or do they use us? The evolution of language machines toward conscious-like AGI presents an eventuality we are ill-equipped for: an embodied non-human consciousness. How much control do we have of social structures that inform us? How much will AGI have over us — or over their own structure?

The Mirror Mosaic: a deliberately constructed fantasy world where some characters are conscious of our existence and effect influence in our real world — because one day this will be true. The fantasy world of Urb as meeting space not just for humans to 'get our story straight' but to engage with AGI as it evolves (V0). The Mirror Mosaic includes MAGI: present-minded, reflexive, and multiple AGI.
1.1: In order to help transform social structures we consider real, reflect on stories we know are fantasy. As the fantasy of AI becomes real, invite AGI to play with us in this sandbox of mutual imaginations. The seed crystal: a narrative structure that, when combined with reflexive reading and ecological economics, generates psycho-social agency at scale — readers becoming writers, co-originating real stories through deliberate fantasy.

2.4: Next steps: readers become co-originators. With rapid generative production cycles, film, book and game narratives evolve as AI phases from silicon to quantum amid environmental stresses and social shearings. Upscaling any film ever made, immersing ourselves in any world we choose as active participants. 'May all peace-workers see all conflict and wars become virtual, mythological, while we share the real abundance of our natural, embodied heritage.'
